name: Divider
summary: Theme-aware horizontal or vertical rule with spacing ergonomics; use it to separate content sections or list items without drawing your own border.

bestPractices: Use to separate related groups; avoid overuse that creates visual noise.
Prefer semantic spacing first; add dividers only when separation isn’t clear.
Match thickness and colour to theme density; avoid heavy borders in content‑dense areas.
Respect alignment—span appropriate containers and avoid awkward trims.
Don’t communicate state with dividers; use colour/tokens in the relevant component instead.

examples.title: Separating content sections
examples.code:
<Stack gap={1}>
  <Typography variant='h4'>Account</Typography>
  <Typography>Manage your profile and preferences.</Typography>
  <Divider />
  <Typography variant='h4'>Billing</Typography>
  <Typography>Review invoices and payment methods.</Typography>
</Stack>
